===''Pokémon the Series''=== ====''[[Pokémon the Series: Sun & Moon]]''==== [[File:Hala anime.png|thumb|250px|Hala in {{aniseries|JN}}]] As revealed in ''[[SM128|League Offenders and Defenders!]]'', Hala was once {{an|Professor Kukui}} and [[Guzma]]'s mentor. He attempted to keep the peace between the pair, but Guzma became disillusioned with Hala's teachings and decided to become strong on his own without using a [[Z-Ring]] or [[Z-Crystal]]s. Hala debuted in ''[[SM009|To Top a Totem!]]'', where he was revealed to be the one who made the Z-Ring that {{DL|Guardian deities (anime)|Tapu Koko}} gave to {{Ash}} in ''[[SM001|Alola to New Adventure!]]''. He tasked Ash with finding a nonviolent way to drive out a group of {{pkmn2|wild}} {{rf|Alolan}} {{p|Rattata}} and {{p|Raticate}}. After Ash gave him the answer, he led Ash to [[Verdant Cavern]], a trial site inhabited by a {{pkmn2|Totem}} {{p|Gumshoos}}, so that Ash could start his [[island challenge]]. Following Ash's victory, he became qualified to take on Hala's grand trial. In ''[[SM010|Trial and Tribulation!]]'', Hala was Ash's opponent in his Melemele grand trial. After a tough battle, Ash emerged victorious. Hala was about to give Ash a [[Fightinium Z]] as a reward, but suddenly, Tapu Koko swooped in and swapped it with an [[Electrium Z]], which Hala gave to Ash instead. In ''[[SM051|Family Determination!]]'', Hala informed Ash and {{ashcl}} of the legend of {{p|Solgaleo}} and the [[Altar of the Sunne]]. In ''[[SM055|The Professors' New Adventure!]]'', he attended the wedding of Professor Kukui and {{an|Professor Burnet}}. In ''[[SM060|Getting a Jump on the Competition!]]'', Hala visited [[Mount Lanakila]], where he and his {{p|Crabrawler}} battled a {{p|Sneasel}} and won, allowing Crabrawler to evolve into {{p|Crabominable}}. Later, along with Crabominable, he entered the [[Pokémon Sled Jump Games]] and won. He reappeared in the [[Poké Problem]] segment of [[SM079]], where he was watching a {{p|Minior}} shower with Hariyama. [[File:Hala younger anime.png|thumb|left|250px|A younger Hala]] In ''[[SM088|Full Moon and Many Arms!]]'', Hala and Hariyama watched the battle between {{an|Lunala}} and {{an|Necrozma}}. Because of Necrozma's presence, he became tired and lacked motivation, but he was eventually restored when Necrozma disappeared. He later prayed at the Ruins of Conflict while he waited for the [[Manalo Festival]] to begin. In ''[[SM090|Securing the Future!]]'', Hala joined the rest of Alola in restoring Necrozma to its {{DL|List of Pokémon with form differences|Necrozma|true form}} by lending it some of his [[Z-Power]]. He reappeared in a flashback in ''[[SM097|No Stone Unturned!]]''. In ''[[SM114|Beauty is Only Crystal Deep!]]'', Hala was working on a Z-Ring when {{DL|Ultra Beasts (anime)|Pheromosa}} arrived at his house. He and Hariyama attempted to battle it, but Pheromosa managed to steal his Fightinium Z before fleeing when the [[Ultra Guardians]] arrived on the scene. In ''[[SM115|The Dealer of Destruction!]]'', Hala, his fellow kahunas, and {{an|Lusamine}} helped Professor Kukui reveal the [[Alola League]] to the public. In ''[[SM116|The Secret Princess!]]'', {{an|Lillie}} visited Hala after she came across [[Mohn|her father]]'s Z-Ring, which he earned from the island kahuna himself years prior. She sought his permission to use the Z-Ring, and Hala accepted on one condition: that Lillie be able to perform a [[Z-Move]] with [[Snowy]]. Lillie took up the challenge, but the Z-Power failed to completely charge up. Even so, Hala was touched by Lillie's ambition to train hard and connect with her father. As a result, he allowed her to keep the Z-Ring temporarily. In ''[[SM125|A Timeless Encounter!]]'', Hala appeared in a flashback, where he defeated an {{tc|Ace Trainer}} while being watched by a young Professor Kukui. [[File:Hala and Hau.png|250px|thumb|Hala and Hau]] In ''[[SM129|Battle Royal 151!]]'', Hala appeared alongside his fellow kahunas, acting as a [[Battle judge|referee]] during the [[Battle Royal]] round of the [[Manalo Conference]]. In ''[[SM130|Battling Besties!]]'', Hala refereed the first pair of first-round matches: one between Ash and {{an|Faba}}, and the other between Guzma and [[Ilima]]—before watching Hau's match against [[Samson Oak]]. In ''[[SM132|Imitation Is the Sincerest Form of Strategy!]]'', Hala watched the battle between Hau and Ash. In [[SM133|the next episode]], [[Nanu]] declared Hau the winner when Hau's {{p|Decidueye}} stood up after a clash with [[Ash's Rowlet]]. However, the verdict was overruled by Hala after he pointed out that Rowlet had fallen asleep instead of being knocked out. Hala later comforted Hau after the latter's loss to Ash. Afterwards, he refereed the battle between {{an|Kiawe}} and {{an|Sophocles}}. Then, from ''[[SM134|The Road to The Semifinals!]]'' to ''[[SM139|Enter the Champion!]]'', he watched the rest of the battles in the stands for the remainder of the tournament. After the final battle ended, Hala attended the award ceremony for the winner, only for it to be interrupted when a {{DL|Ultra Beasts (anime)|Manalo Stadium Guzzlord|Guzzlord}} arrived at the stadium through an [[Ultra Wormhole]]. In ''[[SM140|Z-Move Showdown!]]'', Hala and the other island kahunas joined forces to battle Guzzlord. The guardian deities later came to their assistance, using their powers to keep Guzzlord in place long enough for Hala and the others to fire off their Z-Moves. The combined attacks launched Guzzlord back into the wormhole it came out of, successfully sending it back into [[Ultra Space]]. From ''[[SM141|Exhibition Unmasked!]]'' to ''[[SM144|From Z to Shining Z!]]'', Hala refereed the {{wp|exhibition game|exhibition match}} between Ash and Professor Kukui. Hala appeared in a fantasy in ''[[SM145|Dreams of the Sun and Moon!]]''. In ''[[SM146|Thank You, Alola! The Journey Continues!]]'', Hala acted as the referee of Hau's grand trial battle against Nanu and his {{rf|Alolan}} {{p|Persian}}. ====''[[Pokémon Journeys: The Series]]''==== In ''[[JN112|Helping the Hometown Hero!]]'', Hala hosted a party to welcome {{an|Lillie}} and her family back to Alola after they found Mohn. The next day, Hala watched a [[Battle Royal]] match between {{Ash}}, {{an|Kiawe}}, {{an|Gladion}}, and {{an|Professor Kukui}}. ====Character==== Hala is a carefree individual. He is also respected for his knowledge, experience, and adherence to [[Alola]] traditions. As such, many characters often seek out Hala for advice, like {{an|Lillie}} in ''[[SM116|The Secret Princess!]]''. He holds his position of [[island kahuna]] with immense pride and uses it to instill patience within the younger generations. ====Pokémon==== This listing is of Hala's known {{OBP|Pokémon|species}} in {{aniseries|PTS}}: {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Hala |pkmn=Hariyama |stage1=Makuhita |type1=Fighting |img=Hala Makuhita.png |cap=Hala's Makuhita |img2=Hala Hariyama.png |cap2=Hala's Hariyama |epnum=SM009 |epname=To Top a Totem! |vajp=Shin-ichiro Miki |vaen=Bill Rogers |desc={{p|Hariyama}} appeared in ''[[SM009|To Top a Totem!]]'', where it was shown beside its {{pkmn|Trainer}}. In ''[[SM010|Trial and Tribulation!]]'', it was the second Pokémon Hala used in his battle against {{Ash}}. After a hard-fought battle, it was defeated by {{AP|Pikachu}}'s {{m|Breakneck Blitz}}. It reappeared in ''[[SM051|Family Determination!]]'' at Hala's house, and yet again in ''[[SM055|The Professors' New Adventure!]]'', when it attended the wedding of {{an|Professor Kukui}} and {{an|Professor Burnet}} with Hala. In ''[[SM079|Showering the World with Love!]]'', Hariyama was watching a {{p|Minior}} shower alongside its Trainer. It appeared yet again in ''[[SM088|Full Moon and Many Arms!]]''. In ''[[SM090|Securing the Future!]]'', Hariyama joined the rest of Alola in showering {{an|Necrozma}} with light so it could return to its {{DL|List of Pokémon with form differences|Necrozma|true form}}. It reappeared in a flashback in ''[[SM097|No Stone Unturned!]]''. In ''[[SM114|Beauty is Only Crystal Deep!]]'', Hariyama attempted to battle {{p|Pheromosa}} but lost. In ''[[SM125|A Timeless Encounter!]]'', it appeared as a {{p|Makuhita}} in a flashback. It fought an {{tc|Ace Trainer}}'s {{rf|Alolan}} {{p|Sandslash}} and won. In ''[[SM140|Z-Move Showdown!]]'', Hariyama was used battle a {{DL|Ultra Beasts (anime)|Manalo Stadium Guzzlord|Guzzlord}} that was attacking [[Manalo Stadium]]. It reappeared in a flashback in ''[[SM144|From Z to Shining Z!]]''. Hariyama's known moves are {{m|Fake Out}}, {{m|Knock Off}}, {{m|Arm Thrust}}, {{m|Belly Drum}}, {{m|Heavy Slam}}, and {{m|Close Combat}}.
Hariyama can also perform the [[Z-Move]] {{m|All-Out Pummeling}}.}} {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Hala |pkmn=Crabominable |stage1=Crabrawler |type1=Fighting |type2=Ice |img=Hala Crabrawler.png |cap=Hala's Crabrawler |img2=Hala Crabominable.png |cap2=Hala's Crabominable |epnum=SM010 |epname=Trial and Tribulation! |vajp=Fumiko Takekuma |vaen=Jake Paque |desc=Hala's {{p|Crabominable}} first appeared as a {{p|Crabrawler}} in ''[[SM010|Trial and Tribulation!]]'', being the first Pokémon Hala sent out during his grand trial battle against {{Ash}}. Despite putting up a good fight, it eventually fell to {{AP|Rowlet}}. In ''[[SM055|The Professors' New Adventure!]]'', it attended the wedding of {{an|Professor Kukui}} and {{an|Professor Burnet}} with Hala. In ''[[SM060|Getting a Jump on the Competition!]]'', Crabrawler evolved into Crabominable after defeating a {{p|Sneasel}} on [[Mount Lanakila]]. It later entered the [[Pokémon Sled Jump Games]] along with its Trainer, where their impressive performance granted them first place. It reappeared in a flashback in ''[[SM097|No Stone Unturned!]]''. Crabominable's known moves are {{m|Bubble Beam}}, {{m|Brutal Swing}}, and {{m|Power-Up Punch}}.}} ====Pokémon competitions==== [[File:Hala Sled Jump Games.png|250px|thumb|Hala winning the Pokémon Sled Jump Games]] Hala has competed in the following {{cat|Pokémon competitions}}: * [[Pokémon Sled Jump Games]] - Winner (''[[SM060|Getting a Jump on the Competition!]]'') ====Voice actors==== {{vatable|color={{yellow color light}}|bordercolor={{yellow color}} |ja=檜山修之 ''[[Nobuyuki Hiyama]]'' |pl=Adam Bauman |da=Michael Elo |fi=Jarmo Koski |id=Rudi Sukistiyono (RTV dub) |ru=Олег Куценко ''Oleg Kutzenko'' |en=[[Ryan Andes]] |nl=Huub Dikstaal |pt_br=Isaac Bardavid ({{aniseries|SM}})
